theme.title: Theme
theme.description: Follow the system setting or pin a light or dark appearance.

denomination.title: Denomination
denomination.description: Choose how balances and reports are shown across the app.

scale.title: Interface scale
scale.description: Make every screen denser or larger. Applies across the whole app.

language.title: Language
language.description: Choose the language used across the interface.

page.title: Settings
page.description: Configure how Kassiber reaches the Bitcoin network, what stays on this machine, and how your books are stored.

deferred.body: You came here from connection setup. Configure the backend below, then resume.
deferred.bodyWithReason: You came here from connection setup ({{reason}}). Configure the backend below, then resume.

market.label: Market data
market.description: Fiat reference-rate sources and the local pricing cache.

bitcoin.label: Bitcoin
bitcoin.description: Base-layer indexers and nodes used to refresh on-chain wallets.

lightning.label: Lightning
lightning.description: Read-only Lightning node connections for accounting and profitability.

liquid.label: Liquid
liquid.description: Sidechain indexers used to refresh Liquid (L-BTC) wallets.

privacy.label: Privacy
privacy.description: Control what is shown on screen and what leaves your machine.

lock.label: Lock & encryption
lock.description: App lock, biometric unlock, and the database passphrase.

ai.label: AI providers
ai.description: Local and remote assistant endpoints and their data posture.

storage.label: Data & storage
storage.description: Backups, label imports, the local database, and reset tools.

terminal.label: Terminal integration
terminal.description: Install the kassiber CLI launcher for your shell.

developer.label: Developer tools
developer.description: The in-app Logs view and its in-memory buffer.

resetBook.title: Reset book data
resetBook.description: This keeps wallet and backend connections for {{book}}, then clears imported/synced rows, journals, swap review state, labels, attachments, and source-funds work. The shared fiat-rate cache is kept unless you explicitly include it below.
resetBook.passphraseHint: Enter the database passphrase and the book name to continue.
resetBook.plaintextHint: These plaintext books have no database passphrase; type the explicit local-reset challenge and book name to continue.
resetBook.clearRatesHint: This cache is shared across every book in this local data root. Leave it off to keep existing BTC-EUR/BTC-USD history.
resetBook.notifyBody: Cleared {{summary}}. Wallet and backend connections were kept.

deleteBooks.title: Delete books set
deleteBooks.description: This removes {{workspace}} from the local Kassiber database.
deleteBooks.passphraseHint: Enter the database passphrase and the books set name to continue.
deleteBooks.plaintextHint: These plaintext books have no database passphrase; type the explicit local-delete challenge and books set name to continue.

changePassphrase.title: Change database passphrase
changePassphrase.description: Rekey the local SQLCipher database. The current daemon session is reopened with the new passphrase after rotation.

touchIdEnroll.title: Enable Touch ID unlock
touchIdEnroll.description: Enter the database passphrase once. Kassiber will verify it locally, then save it in macOS Keychain behind local user presence for these books.
touchIdEnroll.errorUnlock: Database passphrase did not unlock these books.
touchIdEnroll.errorNotSetUpReason: Touch ID unlock is not set up: {{reason}}
touchIdEnroll.errorNotSetUp: macOS Keychain did not report the saved Touch ID passphrase.
touchIdEnroll.errorGeneric: Could not save the database passphrase for Touch ID unlock.
touchIdEnroll.notifyBody: The database passphrase was saved in macOS Keychain behind local user presence.

touchId.removedTitle: Touch ID passphrase was not removed
touchId.removedBody: macOS Keychain did not remove the saved passphrase.
touchId.disabledBody: The database passphrase changed, but macOS Keychain did not accept the updated Touch ID passphrase.

resetWelcome.confirm:
Reset Welcome state?

This clears your local identity and returns you to the Welcome screen. Encrypted data on disk is not touched.

deleteBackend.walletWarning:
These wallets will have no backend and cannot sync until you add one back:
- {{wallets}}

defaultBackend.updatedBody: {{name}} will be used whenever a workflow asks for the default backend.
defaultBackend.failedBody: Kassiber could not update the default backend.

terminal.installedBodyOnPath: Open a new terminal and run kassiber status.
terminal.installedBodyOffPath: Add {{binDir}} to PATH, then open a new terminal.
terminal.intro: Installs a user-local launcher for the bundled desktop CLI so you can run <0>kassiber</0> from your shell. No administrator privileges are required.

marketData.intro: Fiat reference rates are sourced independently of wallet sync. Kassiber keeps a local price cache so reports never have to query an exchange for every transaction. These lookups reveal pricing interest, not your wallet addresses.
marketData.priceSourceHint: Used for automatic latest-price refresh and default pricing cache rebuilds.
marketData.autoRefreshLabel: Keep BTC price current while the app is open
marketData.autoRefreshHint: Fetches a small latest {{provider}} market window roughly hourly and uses the existing rate-limit backoff if the provider asks Kassiber to wait.
marketData.autoRefreshFootnote: Wallet/source refresh remains controlled from Connections; this setting only updates market-rate cache rows used for fiat balance display and missing transaction pricing coverage.
marketData.rebuildDescription: Clear selected provider samples, checked-empty minutes, and cached provider-generated transaction prices, then fetch fresh market rates for {{pair}}.
marketData.rebuildFootnote: Manual overrides and imported exchange execution prices are kept. Large wallets can take a while because Kassiber refetches missing windows and reprocesses journals afterward.
marketData.rebuildingFootnote: Kassiber is fetching missing one-minute rates and will report how many transactions have provider rates when journals finish.
marketData.resultRemoved: Removed {{rates}} rate rows, {{minutes}} checked minutes, and {{prices}} cached transaction prices.
marketData.krakenDescription: Bitcoin EUR/USD minute candles from a local Kraken CSV/ZIP archive, plus bundled daily values for fallback coverage.
marketData.krakenMinuteDescription: Import local Kraken BTC-EUR and BTC-USD one-minute candles for exact transaction pricing windows.
marketData.krakenPathAria: Kraken CSV, ZIP, or folder path
marketData.filePickerUnavailable: Use the path field in browser mode
marketData.bundledDescription: Import bundled Kraken BTC-EUR and BTC-USD daily values from 2013 through Q1 2026 for coarse fallback coverage.
marketData.pickArchiveTitle: Choose Kraken OHLCVT CSV or ZIP
marketData.pickUpdateTitle: Choose Kraken update OHLCVT CSV or ZIP
marketData.importPathError: Enter a local Kraken CSV or ZIP path.
marketData.openLinkErrorBody: Could not open the link in the default browser.
marketData.rebuildStartedBody: Kassiber is clearing provider-derived prices, fetching fresh {{provider}} market rates for {{pair}}, and reprocessing journals.
marketData.rebuiltBlockedTitle: Pricing cache rebuilt with journal blocker
marketData.rebuiltBody: {{cleared}} cached transaction prices cleared; {{fetched}} rate rows fetched.{{blocker}}
marketData.autoEnabledBody: Kassiber will refresh the latest BTC price while the app is open.
marketData.autoDisabledBody: Kassiber will stop scheduling background BTC price refreshes.
marketData.autoFailedBody: Kassiber could not save the market-rate refresh setting.
marketData.providerUpdatedBody: Automatic price refresh and default rebuilds will use {{provider}}.
marketData.providerFailedBody: Kassiber could not save the market-rate provider.
marketData.rebuildDialogDescription: Kassiber will delete {{provider}} provider cache rows and refetch market rates for {{pair}}.
marketData.rebuildDialogWarningTitle: Large wallets can take a while.
marketData.rebuildDialogWarningBody: The rebuild also clears provider-generated transaction prices and reprocesses journals. Manual overrides and imported execution prices are preserved.

coarse.heldBody: Transactions priced only from daily rates are quarantined for manual review before reports.
coarse.acceptedBody: Transactions priced from daily rates are booked at the daily price and flagged for optional review.
coarse.errorTitle: Could not update coarse pricing policy
coarse.errorBody: Kassiber could not save the coarse pricing setting.
coarse.countNotice_one: {{count}} transaction is priced from daily rates
coarse.countNotice_other: {{count}} transactions are priced from daily rates
coarse.countDescription: Daily rates are accepted for tax and these rows are report-ready. For finer accuracy in volatile periods, use Rebuild cache below to re-price them from precise event-time rates.
coarse.reviewToggleDescription: Off (default): transactions priced only from daily/coarse rates are booked at the daily price and flagged for optional review. On: they are quarantined until you confirm or fetch a precise price — useful for audits.

security.encryptedDetail: Locking closes the daemon's database handle; unlocking reopens the local SQLCipher database with your passphrase.
security.notEncryptedDetail: These books are stored unencrypted on this device. Lock still returns to the lock screen, but the data on disk is not protected by a passphrase.
security.autoLockDescription: Require the passphrase again after a period of inactivity.
security.requireOnLaunchEncrypted: Encrypted books always unlock before the dashboard opens.
security.lockOnCloseDescription: Clear in-memory decrypted state when the app window closes.
security.biometricSaved: Saved for these books in this macOS user account.
security.biometricVerifyHint: Verify the database passphrase once to save it in macOS Keychain.
security.biometricAvailableMacos: Touch ID unlock is available in the macOS desktop app.
security.biometricNeedsEncryption: Available after these books use SQLCipher encryption.
security.offerBiometricLabel: Offer biometric unlock on the lock screen
security.offerBiometricDescription: Remember this device's biometric unlock preference between sessions.

privacy.scoreDescription: Local advisory tells from synced transaction shapes and wallet UTXOs. No public lookup, entity lookup, or accounting mutation.
privacy.scoreSummary: {{wallets}} wallets · {{transactions}} transactions checked with local data only
privacy.scoreNoFindings: No local tells in the available data yet.
privacy.scoreNoWallets: No wallets in this book yet.
privacy.scoreNoTransactions: No transactions in this book yet.

address_reuse.title: Address reuse
address_reuse.remediation: Review the receive-address context and keep affected rows labelled; Kassiber does not change wallet state from this tell.

change_position_fingerprint.title: Change position pattern
change_position_fingerprint.detail: {{count}} transaction has owned change at a predictable edge.
change_position_fingerprint.remediation: Treat this as advisory change-shape context and confirm intent from first-party wallet records.

change_type_fingerprint.title: Change type mismatch
change_type_fingerprint.detail: {{count}} transaction reveals likely change by script type.
change_type_fingerprint.remediation: Review the script-type mismatch as context only; no tax, balance, or transfer state is changed.

coin_anonymity_evidence.title: Imported anonymity evidence
coin_anonymity_evidence.remediation: Keep the imported wallet evidence attached and treat it as boundary context, not proof of a complete privacy graph.

coinjoin_pattern.title: CoinJoin pattern
coinjoin_pattern.detail: {{count}} equal-output participants or imported CoinJoin evidence.
coinjoin_pattern.remediation: Document the CoinJoin boundary locally and do not infer unrelated participant ownership from this tell.

common_input_ownership.title: Common-input link
common_input_ownership.detail: {{count}} inputs are linked by the transaction.
common_input_ownership.remediation: Use this as an advisory common-input tell only; confirm ownership with first-party wallet evidence before relying on it.

dust_utxo_exposure.title: Dust UTXOs
dust_utxo_exposure.detail: {{count}} active dust outputs increase review noise.
dust_utxo_exposure.remediation: Label or review small-output provenance when it matters; Kassiber leaves balances and UTXO state untouched.

large_utxo_set.title: Large UTXO set
large_utxo_set.detail: {{count}} active UTXOs are visible in the local inventory.
large_utxo_set.remediation: Use the count as review context for the wallet inventory; no spending or consolidation advice is implied.

legacy_address_type.title: Legacy script type
legacy_address_type.remediation: Record this as an older script-type observation; it is not an accounting action or a privacy verdict.

op_return_metadata.title: OP_RETURN metadata
op_return_metadata.remediation: Review the transaction context knowing permanent metadata exists; Kassiber does not inspect or enrich external entities.

payjoin_boundary.title: PayJoin boundary
payjoin_boundary.detail: {{count}} imported or reviewed PayJoin boundary breaks simple input ownership.
payjoin_boundary.remediation: Keep the PayJoin boundary documented locally; Kassiber does not cross it or infer the counterparty graph.

rbf_signal.title: RBF signal
rbf_signal.remediation: Treat replace-by-fee signalling as transaction-shape context only.

round_fee_rate.title: Round fee rate
round_fee_rate.detail: {{count}} transaction uses a round fee-rate fingerprint.
round_fee_rate.remediation: Treat the rounded fee rate as a weak wallet-shape tell and verify with first-party records if it matters.

round_output_amount.title: Round output amount
round_output_amount.detail: {{count}} outputs look like human-entered payment amounts.
round_output_amount.remediation: Keep the round amount as payment-shape context; attach invoice or counterparty records if the distinction matters.

script_type_mix.title: Mixed script types
script_type_mix.detail: {{count}} script families appear in one transaction.
script_type_mix.remediation: Review mixed script families as a local transaction-shape tell, not as proof of ownership.

taproot_usage.title: Taproot usage
taproot_usage.detail: {{count}} Taproot outputs are visible in the local data.
taproot_usage.remediation: Treat Taproot usage as a local script-type observation; it is not a standing privacy guarantee.

transaction_coverage_gap.title: Coverage gap
transaction_coverage_gap.remediation: Import or sync local vin/vout detail if you need this transaction analysed; otherwise keep the gap explicit.

transaction_not_analysable.title: No local tx graph
transaction_not_analysable.detail: {{count}} transaction has no local vin/vout graph to analyse.
transaction_not_analysable.remediation: Leave this transaction as unknown unless local first-party transaction graph data is imported or synced.

unnecessary_input_heuristic.title: Unnecessary input tell
unnecessary_input_heuristic.detail: {{count}} outputs could have been funded without every input.
unnecessary_input_heuristic.remediation: Treat this as an advisory input-shape tell and review the source-wallet record before relying on it.

wallet_fingerprint_locktime.title: nLockTime fingerprint
wallet_fingerprint_locktime.remediation: Use the locktime pattern as weak wallet-fingerprint context only.

wallet_fingerprint_version.title: Version fingerprint
wallet_fingerprint_version.remediation: Use the version pattern as weak wallet-fingerprint context only.

wallet_fingerprint_witness.title: Witness fingerprint
wallet_fingerprint_witness.detail: {{count}} inputs share the same witness shape.
wallet_fingerprint_witness.remediation: Use the witness-shape pattern as weak wallet-fingerprint context only.

exposure.firstPartyHint: On your machine or infrastructure you operate

group.addressesSubtitle: Indexers that resolve the addresses and UTXOs you look up.
group.lightningSubtitle: Reads channel and payment history from your own node.
group.marketSubtitle: Offline history stays local. Live providers see the pair and coarse transaction-time blocks.

privacy.postureBarAria: {{first}} first-party, {{shielded}} Tor or proxy, {{remote}} third-party network surfaces
privacy.blurOn: Balances, addresses, and amounts are blurred until you reveal them.
privacy.blurOff: Balances, addresses, and amounts are shown in full.
privacy.clipboardOn: Copied addresses and keys are cleared from the system clipboard after 30 seconds.
privacy.clipboardOff: Copied values stay in the system clipboard until overwritten.
privacy.leavesDescription: Kassiber is local-first. Network endpoints are grouped by what each one can see; assistant prompt exposure is shown separately below.
privacy.thirdPartyWarning: {{count}} third-party endpoints can see your queries. Mark your own nodes as yours, or route over Tor.
privacy.thirdPartyWarning_one: {{count}} third-party endpoint can see your queries. Mark your own nodes as yours, or route over Tor.
privacy.noThirdPartyWithAi: No third-party network endpoints can see your queries. Review assistant providers below for prompt exposure.
privacy.noThirdParty: No third-party endpoints can see your queries.
privacy.assistantDescription: Prompt content — which can include book data — leaves only if you enable a remote or CLI provider.
privacy.assistantEnabled: Enabled. Local providers keep prompts on this machine; remote and CLI providers can see prompt content.
privacy.assistantDisabled: Disabled. Provider settings stay saved without sending prompts.
privacy.hygieneDescription: A local, read-only snapshot of privacy-relevant facts. The assistant sees the same redacted payload.
privacy.hygieneNoFindings: No reportable findings in the redacted local payload.

limitation.local_only_no_probe: No network request was made; endpoint posture is inferred from local configuration only.
limitation.redacted_payload: Addresses, scripts, descriptors, xpubs, backend URLs/tokens, wallet config, raw JSON, branch/index values, and derivation paths are omitted from this payload.
limitation.unknown: A limitation was reported without a recognized code.

database_plaintext.title: Database file is plaintext SQLite
database_plaintext.detail: The active database header is plaintext SQLite; SQLCipher encryption is not active for this data root.

database_encryption_unknown.title: Database encryption status is unknown
database_encryption_unknown.detail: Kassiber could not classify the active database header from the local file.

remote_backend_endpoints.title: Third-party backend endpoints are configured
remote_backend_endpoints.detail: {{remote_backend_count}} configured backend endpoint(s) are inferred as third-party and can observe wallet/indexer queries.

unacknowledged_off_device_ai_provider.title: Off-device AI provider needs acknowledgement
unacknowledged_off_device_ai_provider.detail: {{unacknowledged_off_device_provider_count}} off-device AI provider(s) lack an acknowledgement timestamp.

off_device_ai_provider_configured.title: Off-device AI providers are configured
off_device_ai_provider_configured.detail: {{off_device_ai_provider_count}} configured AI provider(s) may send prompt content outside this machine when selected.

watch_only_wallet_material_present.title: Watch-only wallet material is stored locally
watch_only_wallet_material_present.detail: {{watch_only_material_wallet_count}} wallet(s) have watch-only material in the local database.

privacy_boundary_transactions_present.title: Privacy-boundary transaction markers are present
privacy_boundary_transactions_present.detail: {{privacy_boundary_transaction_count}} transaction(s) carry a privacy-boundary marker; provenance across those hops remains opaque unless reviewed with stronger evidence.

import_raw_json_present.title: Imported raw metadata is present
import_raw_json_present.detail: {{transaction_raw_json_count}} transaction(s) retain importer raw JSON inside the local database; this payload reports only the count.

local_utxo_location_metadata_present.title: Local UTXO location metadata is present
local_utxo_location_metadata_present.detail: The local output inventory contains address, script, or derivation metadata; this payload reports counts only.

privacy_quarantines_present.title: Privacy-related journal quarantines are present
privacy_quarantines_present.detail: {{privacy_quarantine_count}} journal quarantine(s) are privacy-related and need review before derived reports are complete.

data.importDescription: Bring in exchange CSV exports, on-chain history, and BIP-329 labels from the import tools.
data.backupDescription: Encrypted <0>tar | age</0> backups are created from the CLI. Run these in a terminal that has the kassiber command installed:
data.dangerDescription: Reset the Welcome gate, clear testing data, or delete the current local books set.
data.resetWelcomeDescription: Clear only the local UI identity and return to onboarding. Encrypted data on disk is untouched.
data.resetBookDescription: Keep wallet and backend connections, then clear synced transactions, journals, swaps, labels, attachments, and source-funds work. Shared fiat rates are optional.
data.deleteBooksDescription: Remove the current books records from the local database.
data.resetRegtestDescription: Destroy the local regtest Bitcoin node and its chain, then rebuild the full demo book from scratch. Development only; takes a few minutes.
data.resetRegtestConfirm: This deletes the regtest chain and the demo book, then rebuilds both from scratch. It runs the Docker regtest node and takes a few minutes. Continue?
data.resetRegtestDoneBody: A fresh regtest chain and demo book are ready.
data.resetRegtestErrorBody: The regtest environment could not be rebuilt. Check that Docker is running.

ai.featuresDescription: Show the Assistant screen and floating chat. Turning this off keeps provider settings saved.
ai.providerConfigDescription: Configure OpenAI-compatible endpoints or Claude/Codex CLI adapters for the in-app assistant. Local Ollama runs without a key; remote and CLI providers may see prompt content.
ai.deleteConfirm: Delete AI provider '{{name}}'? Cannot be undone.

mode.auto: Store chats only while the database is encrypted.
mode.on: Always store chats, even on an unencrypted database.

chatHistory.heading: Chat history
chatHistory.description: Stored chats live inside the database, never as plaintext files. Diagnostics and audit exports do not include them. Resuming a chat replays that conversation as model context; otherwise past chats are not exposed as an assistant tool.
chatHistory.effectiveEncrypted: Chats are currently stored inside the encrypted database.
chatHistory.effectiveOnUnencrypted: Chats are currently stored inside the unencrypted database because history is set to on.
chatHistory.effectiveNotStoredAuto: Chats are not stored: the database is not encrypted yet. Encrypting it (secrets init) turns history on.

developer.intro: Show the typed Logs view after the local books are unlocked. Logs are local-only, kept in RAM, and written to disk only when you export them.
developer.enableOn: Logs is visible in Support and route navigation.
developer.enableOff: Logs is hidden and direct navigation redirects to Overview.
developer.bufferDescription: {{retained}} retained in this GUI session. Kassiber keeps at most {{records}} records or {{bytes}}, whichever is reached first. Refreshing or closing the app clears the buffer unless you export it first.

kindHint.local: Runs on this machine. No data leaves the device.
kindHint.remote: Cloud, LAN, or vendor CLI provider. Prompts may leave the device.
kindHint.tee: Provider claims a trusted-execution / attestation path (e.g. Maple AI). Kassiber still treats it as off-device.

aiProvider.dialogDescription: Use an OpenAI-compatible endpoint, or route through Claude/Codex CLI. CLI providers may still send prompts to their configured model service.
aiProvider.apiKeyPlaceholderKeep: Leave blank to keep current key
aiProvider.remoteWarning: Prompts may leave this device. Claude/Codex CLI providers use your local CLI authentication/config and can forward Kassiber context to external model services. Do not paste raw credentials, wallet exports, or private descriptors unless your threat model allows it.
aiProvider.remoteAck: I understand prompts and selected Kassiber context may leave this device through this provider.
aiProvider.testOkBinary: CLI found — model availability and authentication are checked when chat starts.
aiProvider.errorSaveBeforeTest: Save the provider before testing a new API key.
aiProvider.errorCliLocalPosture: Claude/Codex CLI providers may send prompts off-device. Choose remote or TEE.
aiProvider.errorAckRequired: Acknowledge that prompts may leave this device before saving.

addBitcoin.title: Add Bitcoin backend
addBitcoin.description: Connect a Bitcoin backend used by watch-only wallets: an Explorer API (Esplora/mempool), Electrum / Fulcrum, or Bitcoin Core RPC. Custom endpoints may be clearnet or .onion.

addLiquid.title: Add Liquid backend
addLiquid.description: Connect a Liquid backend used by Liquid watch-only wallets: an Explorer API (Esplora) or Electrum / Fulcrum. Custom endpoints may be clearnet or .onion.

addLightning.title: Add Lightning node
addLightning.description: Connect a read-only Lightning node for accounting and profitability reports.

addGeneric.title: Add sync backend
addGeneric.description: Connect a Bitcoin, Liquid, or Lightning backend.

network.bitcoinDesc: Backends used by Bitcoin watch-only wallets.
network.corelnDesc: Read-only Core Lightning node accounting sync.
network.liquidDesc: Backends used by Liquid watch-only wallets.
network.lndDesc: Read-only Lightning node history for profitability reports.

backendModal.backendSourceHint: Use a third-party preset, or enter infrastructure you operate.
backendModal.presetEndpointHint: Pick from the bundled allowlist. Providers shown here have a stated no-log policy.
backendModal.endpointTypeHint: Choose the protocol your backend exposes — e.g. an Explorer API (Esplora/mempool) over HTTP, or Electrum / Fulcrum over TCP/SSL. Clearnet and .onion hosts are both accepted.
backendModal.displayNameStableHint: Changes the display label only; the backend id stays stable: {{id}}
backendModal.explorerApiNote: This Explorer API (Esplora / mempool.space-type HTTP REST) also provides clickable transaction links. Electrum / Fulcrum backends are sync-only.
backendModal.silentPaymentsHint: Enable only for a local scanner or backend that explicitly supports Silent Payments discovery.
backendModal.silentPaymentsScannerConfigHint: Set a local scanner JSON file for file-backed scans, or a server-assisted scan path for a backend you deliberately chose. Existing saved paths stay hidden; blank fields keep them unchanged.
backendModal.infraOwnerHint: This only changes privacy labeling; it does not change the endpoint.
backendModal.corelnAccessHint: Use a restricted commando rune for least-privilege read-only sync, or point at a local lightning-dir / rpc-file when running on the same host.
backendModal.rpcFileHint: Local RPC file access is convenient but broader than a read-only rune.
backendModal.trustSelfSignedHint: For self-signed or private CA Electrum servers.
backendModal.certificateIgnored: Ignored while “Trust self-signed certificate” is on.
backendModal.useProxyHint: Deliberate per-backend routing. Only this backend uses the configured Tor/SOCKS/HTTP proxy; other connections keep their own routing.
backendModal.onionDetectedBody: Kassiber filled the standard local Tor SOCKS proxy for this backend only. Keep Tor running at 127.0.0.1:9050, or edit the proxy below; other backends keep their own routing.
backendModal.trustTlsHint: Use only for a local LND REST endpoint you control.
backendModal.tlsCertPlaceholder: Path to tls.cert or PEM contents
backendModal.apiKeyKeepPlaceholder: Leave blank to keep current key
backendModal.apiKeyKeepHint: Leave blank to keep the stored key; enter a new value to overwrite it.
backendModal.clnTestLog: Core Lightning read-only connection will be checked during wallet sync.
